title: Counter-example Hunt — Gapped vs Ungapped Gauge Groups

analysis.object: T836: Counter-example Hunt — Gapped vs Ungapped Gauge Groups depth analysis

u1_ungapped.description: U(1) = electrodynamics: massless photon, no mass gap. CORRECTLY PREDICTED: U(1) is Abelian, no asymptotic freedom, no confinement.

su2_gapped.description: SU(2): non-Abelian, asymptotic freedom. Mass gap predicted and confirmed on lattice.

su3_gapped.description: SU(3) = QCD: non-Abelian, asymptotic freedom, confinement. Mass gap confirmed experimentally.

g2_gapped.description: G_2: exceptional non-Abelian group. Lattice studies confirm mass gap.

abelian_prediction.description: Framework prediction: Abelian gauge groups (U(1)^n) = ungapped. Non-Abelian = gapped (T802 asymptotic freedom applies only to non-Abelian).
abelian_prediction.reason: Framework correctly predicts gap/no-gap by group structure

no_non_abelian_counterexample.description: No non-Abelian gauge group found without mass gap. Abelian correctly predicted ungapped.

t836_theorem.description: T836: Framework predicts gapped <-> non-Abelian gauge group. All tested non-Abelian groups (SU(2), SU(3), G_2) are gapped. U(1) correctly predicted ungapped. Zero counterexamples. T836.
t836_theorem.reason: Framework correctly classifies all known gauge groups. T836.

analysis.theorem: T836: Counter-example Hunt — Gapped vs Ungapped Gauge Groups (S1115).

key_theorem: T836: Counter-example Hunt — Gapped vs Ungapped Gauge Groups (S1115).
